observations.recommendation: 4) The Accounting Office to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the necessary details for each class of PPE reported in the financial statements, including increases, to support amounts reported in the financial statements.
9) Likewise, have the GSO keep an updated complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
10) Finally, require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

14) The Local Chief Executive shall instruct the BAC Secretariat to regularly furnish the Auditor’s Office with copies of the POs, together with its supporting documents, within five (5) days upon perfection thereof, for evaluation.

14) The Local Chief Executive shall instruct the BAC Secretariat to regularly furnish the Auditor’s Office with copies of the POs, together with its supporting documents, within five (5) days upon perfection thereof, for evaluation.
15) Initiate the keeping of separate updated subsidiary records for each transfer and for future transfers from NGAs, whether or not maintained in separate bank accounts;
16) Make the necessary corresponding adjustments to correct the ledgers, trial balance, and the financial statements; and
17) The immediate submission of all un-submitted reports, if any, pertaining to the aforementioned projects to the Auditor’s Office.
18) Include in the monitoring report of PPAs the physical and financial status/accomplishment of the project.

19) The Local Chief Executive shall instruct the concerned offices to faithfully and regularly submit the monthly pre-closing trial balance, together with SAAOB, and necessary supporting schedules, to the Auditor’s Office not later than the 20th day after the end of the month for evaluation and monitoring purposes.

20) Direct the Accountant to record all regular procurements of supplies in their respective inventory accounts, record the same as expense when issuance thereof takes place, and maintain the appropriate ledgers;

23) Direct the Inventory Committee to validate the same by conducting a semestral physical count of inventory by items. Thereafter, prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the audit team not later than July 31, and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

24) The Local Chief Executive shall instruct the concerned office to disclose explanation of differences reported in the expenditure portion of the Statement of Comparison on Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) in the notes to financial statements.

26) Instruct that the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Funds, upon submission by the MDRRM Officer, be supported with documentation of the programs/projects/and activities undertaken such as but not limited to the following: photocopy of the LDRRMF Investment Plan, copies of approved resolution for the release and utilization of QRF and Special Trust Fund, during state of calamities, and photographs of projects/programs/ activities undertaken during the month of report.
28) Finally, include in the Notes to the Financial Statements a discussion on the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the prescribed format.
31) Prepare a schedule of cancelled checks at the end of the month, attach the actual cancelled checks, and regularly submit the same along with the RCI to the Auditor’s Office.
32) The Municipal Treasurer to observe the stamping of “PAID”, with the prescribed size of 2’’ x 3’’, on all the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents after payment is made.

33) The number of check issued and the date of the actual payment shall be indicated on the face of each of the supporting documents to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in payment of future claims.
34) The Accounting Office shall ensure that all paid DVs, including its supporting documents, pending submission to the Auditor’s Office are stamped “PAID”.
40) Finally, direct the municipal accountant to immediately set up the RPT receivable account upon receipt of the list from the treasurer pursuant to the aforementioned provision.

Therefore, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive mandate that:
a) The pictures of the supplies be taken upon its acceptance and
inspection to affirm its delivery and receipt, and the same be
attached as a supporting document for payment thereof;
b) The recording of all purchased supplies and materials be coursed
through its respective inventory accounts upon acceptance and
inspection thereof;
c) The issuance of all supplies and materials be supported with RIS;
d) The (weekly) SSMI be prepared by the Municipal GSO based
from the RISs;
e) A JEV be prepared by the Municipal Accountant to record
issued/expensed supplies based on the SSMI;
f) The Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards be completely
maintained by the Municipal GSO and the Municipal
Accountant, respectively, by stock number, and the same be
regularly reconciled; and
g) The semestral RPCI be prepared based on the results of the
thorough physical count of inventories and thereupon be
submitted to the Auditor’s Office not later than July 31 and
January 31 of each year.

2.8 Hence, we recommended and Management agreed to mandate:
a) The evaluation of composition of the Inventory Committee at the
beginning of the year, if warranted, replace and/or augment the
members with active and trained personnel who can conduct the
physical count effectively;
b) The Inventory Committee to prepare a PIP, to be approved by the
LCE, which shall guide them in conducting and completing the
annual physical count of all PPEs;
c) The Inventory Committee to assign prescribed unique property
numbers on existing PPEs during the physical count and the
Municipal GSO to do the same on newly procured properties and
equipment;
d) The Inventory Committee to identify the actual condition of the
properties counted and include its status in the report;
e) The Inventory committee to submit the RPCPPE within the
deadline set by the Commission to the Auditor’s Office not later
than January 31 of each year;
f) The complete keeping, maintenance and regular reconciliation of
the property cards, and the PPELC with the results of the physical
count; and
g) Lastly, pending the approval of the supplemental guidelines for
COA Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 on the increase in
capitalization threshold from 15,000.00 to 50,000.00 of tangible
items, we recommended that all existing equipment, especially
those below ₱50,000.00, be identified, immediately assigned with
prescribed unique property numbers, and listed in a schedule, in
preparation for reclassification once the supplemental guidelines
is approved.

3.9 Therefore, we reiterated our prior year recommendations that the
Local Chief Executive enjoin the department heads’ compliance with
the Circular and direct:
a) The Inventory Committee to conduct an extensive inventory of the
road network system under the LGU’s jurisdiction and prepare a
report thereon in accordance with templates provided in COA
Circular No. 2015-008;
b) The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal GSO with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects;
c) The General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain a local road
inventory, road map and keep a complete and updated Local
Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components;
d) The Municipal Accountant to prepare/adjust the Journal Entry
Voucher, as necessary, to record the beginning/adjusted balance
of the local road network and its components in the general ledger
and maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Cards and
subsidiary records for all roads and road components based on
the result of the physical count;
e) The Municipal Accountant to prepare a monthly lapsing schedule
for computation of the depreciation at the end of each year; and
f) The submission of the following to the Auditor’s Office at the end
of the accounting period, preferably not later than January 31 of
each year:
   i. Report on Local Road Network by the GSO;
   ii. Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System
   by the Inventory Committee; and
   iii. Disclosure of Local Road Network System in the Notes to
   Financial Statements by the Accounting Office.

4.10 Hence, we reiterated our prior year recommendations that the Local
Chief Executive direct:
a) The Accounting Office to take up depreciation on the
undepreciated PPEs and make the corresponding adjustments on
affected asset, expense, and government equity accounts by
drawing necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to correct the
misstatements;
b) The General Services Office and Accounting Office to strictly
recognize and record depreciation in the property cards and
ledgers for all existing and to be acquired PPEs;
c) The Accounting Office to prepare a monthly schedule of
depreciation for all the PPE assets of the LGU; and
d) The Accounting Office to disclose in the Notes to Financial
Statements the necessary details for each class of PPE reported in
the financial statements, including increases and derecognition
thereof, if any, to to make the financial statements more
comprehensible.

5.8 Thus, we reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local
Chief Executive compel the:
a) LDRRMO to attach the required documents needed to support
the release of LDRRMF funds and to substantiate the monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Funds with
documentation such as photos of the programs/projects/and
activities undertaken;
b) The Inventory Committee to include in the physical count and in
the RPCPPE the composition of the DRRM equipment;
c) The Accounting Office to prepare and maintain the subisidiary
ledgers and reconcile the same against the RPCPPE; and
d) The on-time submission of the MONTHLY Report of Sources and
Utilization of LDRRM to the Auditor’s Office on or before the 15th
day of the ensuing month.

6.11 Thus, we reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local
Chief Executive direct:
a) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
submit to the Auditor’s Office a schedule showing all the fund
transfers received by the Management as of date, and another
schedule showing the breakdown of the CIP-Infrastructure
account;
b) The Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Entry
Vouchers (JEVs) to establish the trust liability account based on
the submitted schedule to correct the ledgers;
c) The Municipal Accountant to keep separate subsidiary records
for each existing fund transfer and for future fund transfers from
sources agencies;
d) The Municipal Accountant to submit the RCIs and RODs related
to the fund transfers, together with its vouchers and supporting
documents to the Auditor’s Office within ten (10) days from the
end of the month;
e) The MPDO to submit to the Auditor’s Office the list of all on-
going and to be implemented PPAs, properly categorized whether
under the General or under the Trust Fund, at the beginning of
the year; and
f) The Municipal Engineer and/or the MPDO to include in the
monthly monitoring report of PPAs the physical and financial
status of projects funded through fund transfers.

7.7 Thus, we recommended and Management agreed to direct:
a) The Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)
to correct and segregate the current and non-current portion of
the loan and disclose the details of the loan in the Notes to
Financial Statements; and
b) The Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to us the interest
and amortization schedule to verify the correctness of the
amortization and interest payments as well as the correctness of
the total balance at year end.

8.5 Therefore, we recommended and Management concurred to direct:
a) The Municipal Treasurer to follow-up and promptly request from
the AGDB the release of bank statements of all accounts within
five (5) days at the end of the month; and
b) Thereupon, furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant who
shall prepare and submit the BRS, which shall be submitted to the
Auditor’s Office on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month.
The Accountant should also see to it that prior to submission, the
BRS, for each account under each fund, are all supported with
bank statements.

9.3 Thus, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
a) The Treasurer’s Office to submit the copies of the RPT official
receipts together with a schedule showing a summary of the RPT
and SEF collected, and adopt the same for the ensuing years; and
b) The Accounting Office to immediately draw the necessary
Journal Entry Voucher (JEVs) to adjust and correct the affected
accounts.

10.4 Therefore, we recommended and Management concurred to mandate:
a) The HRMO to submit a complete listing of all the JOs hired by
the Management, by department, and with information on age
and period of contract;
b) All the JOs to submit their DTRs or certification of services
rendered duly signed by the immediate supervisor,
Accomplishment Reports duly approved and signed by the
immediate supervisor, and copies of contracts on which the
Statements of Duties and Responsibilities to be attached to and as
basis for the preparation of payroll. Otherwise, payments thereof
maybe suspended or disallowed, if warranted.

11.6 Thus, we recommended and the Management agreed to compel:
a) The Municipal Treasurer to promptly submit all the financial
reports to the Municipal Accountant, together with the DVs and
supporting documents, for recording in the books of accounts;
and
b) The Municipal Accountant, after recording the reports in the
books of accounts, submit the same to the Auditor’s Office not
later the 10th day of the following month.

1) Cause the immediate tracing of the initial recognition of the infrastructure assets and compute the depreciation thereof based on the latest estimated useful life of assets provided by the Commission

2) The Accounting Office to take up the necessary corresponding adjustments on affected accounts such as PPE asset, expense, and government equity account to lessen the materiality of misstatements by recognizing depreciation, especially on newly constructed and newly procured PPEs.
3) The General Services Office and Accounting Office to strictly record depreciation in the related property cards and ledgers, respectively; and
5) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct immediately an extensive inventory of the road network system under the Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare a report thereon

6) Require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officers to religiously update their respective records for roads and road components and make at least a reconciliation of records once a year
7) Further require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule for computation of the depreciation at the end of each year
8) Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to religiously maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map

11) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and meticulously post the acquisition of PPE to the respective ledger cards and property cards upon receipt of the source documents and keep and maintain the same in accordance with the provisions of the Manual
12) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each year;

13) That upon completion of the physical count, the GSO and Accounting Office undertake collaborative effort to maintain, update, and reconcile their cards and ledgers with the results of the physical count; and
21) Direct the General Services Officer to religiously maintain the necessary inventory and supplies’ stock cards and reconcile it with the Accounting records on a regular basis
22) Direct the two offices to religiously reconcile records with the ledgers on a regular basis

25) Instruct the religious and time preparation of the report by the Local Accountant on a monthly basis duly noted by the MDRRMO and regular submission of the report on sources and utilization not later than the 15th day after each month
27) Further instruct the Accountant to establish the Trust Liability-DRRM account as prescribed by the Commission
29) Faithfully submit at the beginning of the year the list of all-on going PPAs and those to be implemented, with proper details as mentioned in COA Circular 2013-004; and
30) Clearly stamp the word “Cancelled” on the face of the cancelled checks

35) Instruct the Accountant to faithfully inspect and review records whether the previous cash advances have been liquidated and properly accounted for prior to the granting of the succeeding cash advance
36) Instruct the Treasurer religiously liquidate the cash advance within the prescribed period and in accordance with the rules and regulations provided by the Commission.
37) Direct the immediate submission of the MSWMP to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for review and for approval by the NSWMC
38) Direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to review the same every two years or as the need arises for purposes of harmonizing the LGU’s programs/projects with the mandate of RA 9003.

39) Direct the municipal treasurer to prepare and submit the updated list of duly certified name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the accountant at the beginning of the year

41) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together with all the supporting liquidating instruments every 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the NGAS for LGUs.

1.9 Hence, we strongly recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct:
a) The Accounting Office to take up the necessary corresponding
adjustments on affected accounts such as PPE asset, expense, and
government equity account to lessen the materiality of
misstatements by recognizing depreciation, especially on new
constructed and newly procured PPEs.
b) The General Services Office and Accounting Office to faithfully
record depreciation in the related property cards and ledgers,
respectively; and
c) The Accounting Office to disclose in the Notes to Financial
Statements the necessary details for each class of PPE reported in
the financial statements, including increases, to support amounts
reported in the financial statements.

a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct immediately
extensive physical count of the road network system under the
Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare a report thereon in
accordance with templates provided in COA Circular 2015-008;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to religiously update the
subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road
network based on the result of the physical count;
c) Further require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing
schedule for computation of the depreciation at the end of each
year;
d) Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to religiously
maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
e) Likewise, have the GSO keep an updated complete Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
f) Finally, require the Municipal Engineer to provide the
Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road
projects.

3.6 Hence, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct:
a) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and meticulously
post the acquisition of PPE to the respective ledger cards and
property cards upon receipt of the source documents and keep
and maintain the same in accordance with the provisions of the
Manual;
b) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed
physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the
RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each
year.
c) That upon completion of the physical count, the General Services
Office and Accounting Office undertake collaborative effort to
maintain, update, and reconcile their cards and ledgers with the
results of the physical count.

4.4 Thus, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor compel the BAC
Secretariat to regularly furnish the Auditor’s Office with copies of the
POs, together with its supporting documents, within five (5) days upon
perfection thereof, for evaluation.

5.4 In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we strongly recommended that the
Honorable Mayor instruct the following:
a) Initiate the keeping of separate updated subsidiary records for
each transfer and for future transfers from NGAs, whether or not
maintained in separate bank accounts;
b) Make the necessary corresponding adjustments to correct the
ledgers, trial balance, and the financial statements; and
c) The immediate submission of all un-submitted reports, if any,
pertaining to the aforementioned projects to the Auditor’s Office.
d) Include in the monitoring report of PPAs the physical and
financial status/accomplishment of the project.

6.4 Hence, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the
concerned offices to faithfully and regularly submit the monthly pre-
closing trial balance, together with SAAOB, and necessary supporting
schedules, to the Auditor’s Office not later than the 20th day after the
end of the month for evaluation and monitoring purposes.

7.3 Therefore, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the
concerned office to disclose explanation of differences reported in the
SCBAA in the notes to financial statements.

8.5 Hence, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct:
a) The immediate submission of the Report on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRM Funds for the months of October to
December 2021;
b) On-time preparation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of
und LDRRMF by the Local Accountant on a monthly basis, duly
noted by the MDRRM Officer, and regular submission thereof to
the Auditor’s Office on or before the 15th day after the end of each
month.
c) We also recommended that the monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRM Funds, upon submission by the MDRRM
Officer, be supported with documentation of the
programs/projects/and activities undertaken such as but not
limited to the following: photocopy of the LDRRMF Investment
Plan, copies of approved resolution for the release and utilization
of QRF and Special Trust Fund, during state of calamities, and
photographs of projects/programs/activities undertaken during
the month of report.

9.7 In view thereof, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct
the MPDO to:
a) Faithfully submit at the beginning of the year the list of all on-
going PPAs and those to be implemented, with proper details as
mentioned in COA Circular 2013-004; and
b) Henceforth, prepare and regularly submit the monthly
monitoring report on the status of PPAs and quarterly report on
the utilization of 20% Development Fund to the Auditor’s Office.

10.4 We recommended and the Honorable Mayor concurred to enjoin the
Office of the Treasurer to:
a) Clearly stamp the word “Cancelled” on the face of the cancelled
checks; and
b) Prepare a schedule of cancelled checks at the end of the month,
attach the actual cancelled checks, and regularly submit the same
along with the RCI to the Auditor’s Office.

11.6 In view thereof, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct
the GAD Focal Person to:
a) Prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report CY 2021 based on the
format prescribed in PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-
01 and submit the same to the Auditor’s Office immediately; and
b) Henceforth, faithfully adhere to the timely submission of the GAD
AR, together with the GAD Plan and Budget, within five (5)
working days from the end of January of the preceding year.

12.5 We recommended and Management concurred that:
a) The Municipal Treasurer shall observe the stamping of “PAID”,
with the prescribed size of 2’’ x 3’’, on all the disbursement
vouchers and its supporting documents after payment is made.
b) The number of check issued and the date of the actual payment
shall be indicated on the face of each of the supporting documents
to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in
payment of future claims.
c) The Accounting Office shall ensure that all paid DVs, including
its supporting documents, pending submission to the Auditor’s
Office are stamped “PAID”.
1) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct immediately an extensive inventory of the road network system under the Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare a report thereon;

2) Require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officers to religiously update their respective records for roads and road components and make at least a reconciliation of records once a year;
3) Further require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule for computation of the depreciation at the end of each year;
4) Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to religiously maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
5) Likewise, have the GSO keep an updated complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
6) Finally, require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

7) Cause the immediate tracing of the initial recognition of the infrastructure assets and compute the depreciation thereof based on the latest estimated useful life of assets provided by the Commission;
8) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up corresponding prior year’s adjustments in the books to be closed to the Government Equity; and
9) Henceforth, ensure that the Municipal Accountant recognizes depreciation expenses on all PPE accounts.

10) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and meticulously post the acquisition of PPE to the respective ledger cards and property cards upon receipt of the source documents and keep and maintain the same in accordance with the provisions of the Manual;
11) Mandate the GSO to retain and keep copies of the AIR, PAR, and ARE as prescribed by the Commission; and
12) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each year.
13) The Local Chief Executive (LCE) shall cause the immediate submission of the approved supplemental AIP to the audit team to facilitate assessment on the validity and propriety of PPAs implemented
14) To submit certified true copies of certifications of posting requirements by the BAC Secretariat relative to the BGCM PPA procurements;
15) To immediately submit the lacking monthly reports on the BGCM fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs;
16) Though late, still post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s website or Facebook page and in conspicuous public places in the locality for general transparency; and
17) Finally, furnish the audit team a copy of the written notice when posting has been complied, including photos to validate posting in conspicuous places in the locality.
18) Submit the aforementioned lacking supporting documents for evaluation by the audit team;

19) Furnish the audit team an authenticated copy of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with DOH CHDs for the transfer of funds and the payment of the COVID-19 related grants to eligible frontline workers as required in Section 6.1.2.b of DBM-DOH Joint Circulars No. 1 and 2 (if any);
20) Furnish the audit team with authenticated copies of the reports and certifications submitted to DOH-CHDs as required in Section 6.4.b to 6.4.d of the same joint circular (if any); and

21) Henceforth, complete the necessary supporting documents before payment of any transaction, draw the necessary journal entries, regularly attach the approved JEVs to disbursement vouchers, and submit the reports, complete, and within the prescribed timeframe to the audit team.

22) Direct the Accountant to record all regular procurements of supplies in their respective inventory accounts, record the same as expense when issuance thereof takes place, and maintain the appropriate ledgers;
23) Direct the General Services Officer to religiously maintain the necessary inventory and supplies’ stock cards and reconcile it with the Accounting records on a regularly basis;
24) Direct the two offices to religiously reconcile records with the ledgers on a regular basis;

25) Direct the Inventory Committee to validate the same by conducting a semestral physical count of inventory by items. Thereafter, prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the audit team not later than July 31, and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

26) The Local Chief Executive shall ensure that appropriations on budgetary requirements, such as the LDRRMF are strictly complied with and in accordance with the law before approval of the ordinance.
27) Cause the immediate submission of the copy of the approved Local Sanggunian Resolution on the release and utilization of the QRF to the audit team;
28) Strictly instruct that the LDRRMO religiously keep, maintain, and update the three (3) RAAOs in accordance with the rules of the Commission;
29) Instruct the religious and regular submission of the report on sources and utilization not later than the 15th day after each month;

30) Instruct the Inventory Committee and the LDRRMO to immediately do an extensive physical count of all the equipment under the MDRRMO and consequently submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE to the audit team;
31) Instruct the Accountant to draw appropriate journal entries to record or reclassify the equipment according to the results of the physical count;
32) Further instruct the Accountant to establish the Trust Liability-DRRM account as prescribed by the Commission; and
33) Finally, include in the Notes to the Financial Statements a discussion on the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the prescribed format.
34) Cause the immediate submission of the duly approved APP and PPMPs to the audit team for evaluation;
35) Henceforth, instruct that PPMPs and APP be religiously prepared and complied in accordance with Section 7 Rule II of RA 9184; and
36) Ensure that all procurements are in accordance with the duly approved APP.

37) Instruct the Accountant to faithfully inspect and review records whether the previous cash advances have been liquidated and properly accounted for prior to the granting of the succeeding cash advance;
38) Instruct the Accountant that after verification that are no cash advances pending liquidation, issue a certification thereof prior to the granting of succeeding cash advance; and
39) Instruct the Treasurer to religiously liquidate the cash advance within the prescribed period and in accordance with the rules and regulations provided by the Commission.
40) Direct the immediate submission of the MSWMP to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for review and for approval by the NSWMC; and
41) Direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to review the same every two years or as the need arises for purposes of harmonizing the LGU’s programs/projects with the mandate of RA 9003.

42) The Honorable Mayor shall instruct the accountant to regularly maintain and update separate registries required by the circular to ensure proper monitoring and utilization of LDRRM Fund in accordance with rules and regulations.

43) Direct the municipal treasurer to prepare and submit the updated list of duly certified name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the accountant at the beginning of the year; and
44) Finally, direct the municipal accountant to immediately set up the RPT receivable account upon receipt of the list from the treasurer pursuant to the aforementioned provision.

45) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together with all the liquidating instruments every 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the NGAS for LGUs.

16. Direct the municipal treasurer to prepare and submit the updated list of duly certified name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the accountant at the beginning of the year; and
17. Finally, direct the municipal accountant to immediately set up the RPT receivable account upon receipt of the list from the treasurer pursuant to the aforementioned provision.

1.6 Therefore, we highly reiterated our previous year’s recommendation
that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct immediately
an extensive inventory of the road network system under the
Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare a report thereon;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services
Officer to religiously update their respective records for roads
and road components and make at least a reconciliation of
records once a year;
c) Further require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing
schedule for computation of the depreciation at the end of each
year;
d) Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to religiously
maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
e) Likewise, have the GSO keep an updated complete Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
f) Finally, require the Municipal Engineer to provide the
Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road
projects.

2.7 Therefore, we highly recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Cause the immediate tracing of the initial recognition of the
infrastructure assets and compute the depreciation thereof based
on the latest estimated useful life of assets provided by the
Commission;
b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up corresponding
prior years’ adjustment in the books to be closed to the
Government Equity account; and
c) Henceforth, ensure that the Municipal Accountant recognizes
depreciation expense on all PPE accounts.

3.9 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE), strictly:
a) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and meticulously
post the acquisition of PPE to the respective ledger cards and
property cards upon receipt of the source documents and keep
and maintain the same in accordance with the provisions of the
Manual;
b) Mandate the GSO to retain and keep copies of the AIR, PAR,
and ARE as prescribed by the Commission; and
c) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed
physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the
Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment
(RPCPPE) to the audit team not later than January 31, of each
year.

3.10 The Management submitted their RPCPPE on March 8, 2021. However,
PPEs were not elaborated as prescribed. During the exit conference, the
audit team emphasized that the conduct of physical count of all PPEs
should be extensive. Consequently, the RPCPPE should be prepared in a
detailed manner. Each category of equipment should be enumerated per
item and specifically identified by property number. Likewise, land
improvements and infrastructure assets should also be enumerated by
item, indicating the location and specifications.

5.6 In view of the noted deficiencies, we highly recommended that the
Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance Committee:
a) To submit certified true copies of certifications of posting
requirements by the BAC Secretariat relative to the BGCM PPA
procurements;
b) To immediately submit the lacking monthly reports on the
BGCM fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs;
c) Though late, still post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s
website or Facebook page and in conspicuous public places in
the locality for general transparency; and
d) Finally, furnish the audit team a copy of the written notice when
posting has been complied, including photos to validate posting
in conspicuous places in the locality.

6.9 Hence, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly direct
the concerned personnel to:
a) Submit the aforementioned lacking supporting documents for
evaluation by the audit team;
b) Furnish the audit team an authenticated copy of the
Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with DOH CHDs for the
transfer of funds and the payment of the COVID-19 related
grants to eligible frontline workers as required in Section 6.1.2.b
of DBM-DOH Joint Circulars No. 1 and 2 (if any);
c) Furnish the audit team with authenticated copies of the reports
and certifications submitted to DOH-CHDs as required in
Section 6.4.b to 6.4.d of the same joint circular (if any); and
d) Henceforth, complete the necessary supporting documents
before payment of any transaction, draw the necessary journal
entries, regularly attach the approved JEVs to disbursement
vouchers, and submit the reports, complete, and within the
prescribed timeframe to the audit team.

7.9 Due to the foregoing lapses noted, we highly recommended that the
Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Accountant to record all regular procurements of
supplies in their respective inventory accounts, record the same
as expense when issuance thereof takes place, and maintain the
appropriate ledgers;
b) Direct the General Services Officer to religiously maintain the
necessary inventory and supplies’ stock cards and reconcile it
with the Accounting records on a regular basis;
c) Direct the two offices to religiously reconcile records with the
ledgers on a regular basis;
d) Direct the Inventory Committee to validate the same by
conducting a semestral physical count of inventory by items.
Thereafter, prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) to the audit team not later than July 31, and
January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters,
respectively.

9.14 Hence, we strongly recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Cause the immediate submission of the copy of the approved
Local Sanggunian Resolution on the release and utilization of the
QRF to the audit team;
b) Strictly instruct that the LDRRMO religiously keep, maintain,
and update the three (3) RAAOs in accordance with the rules of
the Commission;
c) Instruct the regular submission of the report on sources and
utilization not later than the 15th day after each month;
d) Instruct the Inventory Committee and the LDRRMO to
immediately do an extensive physical count of all the equipment
under the MDRRMO and consequently submit the Report on
the Physical Count of PPE to the audit team;
e) Instruct the Accountant to draw appropriate journal entries to
record or reclassify the equipment according to the results of the
physical count;
f) Further instruct the Accountant to establish the Trust Liability-
DRRM account as prescribed by the Commission; and
g) Finally, include in the Notes to the Financial Statements a
discussion on the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the
prescribed format.

10.4 We therefore recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Cause the immediate submission of the duly approved APP and
PPMPs to the audit team for evaluation;
b) Henceforth, instruct that PPMPs and APP be religiously
prepared and complied in accordance with Section 7 Rule II of
RA 9184; and
c) Ensure that all procurements are in accordance with the duly
approved APP.

11.7 Therefore, steadfast, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive:
a) Instruct the Accountant to faithfully inspect and review records
whether the previous cash advances have been liquidated and
properly accounted for prior to the granting of the succeeding
cash advance;
b) Instruct the Accountant that after verification that no cash
advances are pending liquidation, issue a certification thereof
prior to the granting of succeeding cash advance; and
c) Instruct the treasurer to religiously liquidate the cash advance
within the prescribed period and in accordance with the rules
and regulations provided by the Commission.

4. We recommended that Management widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three (3) priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
5. We recommended further that the Honorable Mayor direct the concerned officials to identify in their reports which local roads are rehabilitated to avoid loss from repetition of projects.

6. Direct the immediate submission of the MSWMP to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for review and for approval by the NSWMC; and
7. Direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to review the same every two years or as the need arises for purposes of harmonizing the LGU’s programs/projects with the mandate of RA 9003.

8. We recommended that direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDDRMO) to prepare and submit Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the LDDRM Council.
9. We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in compliance with the above quoted rules.

10. We recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the accountant to regularly maintain and update the separate registries required by the circular to ensure proper monitoring and utilization of LDRRM Fund in accordance with rules and regulations.
11. Further, to strengthen the need for the submission of the LDRRMFIP, we recommended that the said plan be regularly and strictly attached as supporting documents whenever disbursements are made hereafter, pursuant to the above stated provision to ensure that expenditures are in accordance with the approved LDRRMFIP.

12. The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check in the amount of P2,698,572.06 with complete supporting documents for the fund transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund from General Fund to Special Trust Fund; and
13. The Municipal Account to record the transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the appropriate accounting entries under General and Trust Fund.

14. Strictly impose on the municipal treasurer the daily preparation of the RCDs, duly supported by the necessary documents such as copies of the official receipts and validated deposit slips; and
15. Direct the municipal accountant to review the RCDs, together with its attachments, submitted by the municipal treasurer and record the appropriate JEVs on a regular basis, with accuracy, to extinguish doubt on the financial statements.

21. We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat and other concerned division officials to furnish the copies of the contracts and purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after its perfection together with all its necessary supporting documents pursuant COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

22. We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.
23. We further recommended that for the CY 2019, the GAD Plan and Budget should be prepared and submitted immediately to the Audit Team.

24. We recommended that Management ensure that the Accountant prepare and submit to the Auditor monthly the BRS together with the original copies of the debit/credit memo pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for timely verification of the cash in bank account.
25. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a General/Subsidiary Ledger for the account Cash in Bank per fund and record on it all the transactions affecting the said account for proper monitoring of the availability of cash at a given time.

26. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) on a monthly basis in its prescribed form.
27. We further recommended that Management require the inventory of the accountable forms for official receipts, prepare its RAAF and submit it immediately to the Auditor.

28. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: strictly comply with the provisions of the law in the preparation and submission of Reports of Collections and Deposits and its supporting documents like the Official Receipts and Validated Deposit Slips on a monthly basis.

29. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together with all the supporting liquidating instruments every 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the NGAS for LGUs.
30. Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately review and evaluate the submitted RODs and its attachments and then prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers for submission to the Auditor.
31. Finally, due to the unreasonable delay, require the immediate submission of all the reports to the Office of the Auditor and the prompt submission thereafter.

32. Create and Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count and require the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to update their ledger cards and make the necessary reconciliation thereof.

1. We recommended that
Management widen the
allocation of utilization of 20%
development fund to the three
(3) priority programs and
projects such as social
development, economic
development and
environment management pursuant to
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1
dated February 22, 2017.

1.5 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
    a. Create the Inventory Committee of the municipality to
    conduct the inventory on the physical count of its road
    network and prepare a report thereon;
    b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local roads
    network accounts based on the result of the physical count;
    and
    c. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant
    and the General Services Office with the complete description
    and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

2.5 In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our previous year’s
recommendation that management widen the allocation of utilization
of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects
such as social development, economic development and
environment management pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

3.5 Hence, we recommended that Honorable Mayor:
    a) Direct the immediate submission of the MSWMP to the Provincial
    Solid Waste Management Board for review and for approval by
    the NSWMC; and
    b) Direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to review
    the same every two years or as the need arises for purposes of
    harmonizing the LGU’s programs/projects with the mandate of
    RA 9003.

4.7 Hence, we rigidly reiterated our previous year’s recommendation
that the Honorable Mayor strictly oversee the preparation and
submission of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) by the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (LDDRMO) based on the LDRRM
Plan and Budget approved by the LDDRM Council; and

4.8 Direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRMO
to prepare and submit the Report on the Utilization of DRRMF on a
regular monthly basis, which is on or before the 15th day after the
end of each month, in compliance with the above quoted rules.

5.5 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the accountant
to regularly maintain and update the separate registries required by
the circular to ensure proper monitoring and utilization of LDRRM
Fund in accordance with rules and regulations.

6.8 In view of the foregoing, we highly recommended that the Honorable
Mayor:
    a) Strictly impose on the municipal treasurer the daily preparation
    of the RCDs, duly supported by the necessary documents such
    as copies of the official receipts and validated deposit slips; and
    b) Direct the municipal accountant to review the RCDs, together
    with its attachments, submitted by the municipal treasurer and
    record the appropriate JEVs on a regular basis, with accuracy, to
    extinguish doubt on the financial statements.

7.4 Therefore, we highly recommended that the Mayor strictly instruct
the keeping of an updated real property tax account register to the
concerned officials;
    a) Direct the municipal treasurer to prepare and submit the
    updated list of duly certified name of taxpayers and the
    amount due and collectible for the year to the accountant at
    the beginning of the year; and
    b) Finally, direct the municipal accountant to immediately set up
    the RPT receivable account upon receipt of the list from the
    treasurer pursuant to the aforementioned provision.

8.9 Hence, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
    a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials
    to address and comply with the deficiencies noted during the
    validation and submit an updated copy of the same to the
    Auditor for review;
    b) Moreover, require the same to strictly comply with the recording,
    updating, and maintaining of monthly accounting records,
    onwards;
    c) Direct the same to conscientiously keep and file copies of the all
    the monthly reports submitted to the auditor.

9.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
    a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit separate ROD for
    each of the various cash advances together with all the
    supporting liquidating instruments to the Municipal Accountant
    immediately after purpose of the cash advance has been served;
    and
    b) Require the appropriate officials to submit their monthly
    mandatory reports such as the RCIs, together with its necessary
    attachments, RAAF, and other reports required, strictly within
    the time frame prescribed by the Commission;

12.5 Hence, we recommended that LGU exhaust all available remedies
provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing
immediate compliance with the audit suspensions pursuant to
Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009.

We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor:

together with the original

18. Create and Inventory
Committee to conduct the

Municipal Accountant and the
General Service Officer to

stock cards and make the
necessary reconciliation
thereof.

We recommended that Management widen the allocation of
utilization of 20% development fund to the three (3) priority
programs and projects such as social development, economic
development and environment management pursuant to DILG-
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated
February 22, 2017.

We recommended that Management direct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (LDDRMO) to prepare and
submit Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) based on the LDRRM Plan and
Budget approved by the LDDRM Council.

We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Accountant in coordination with the MDRRMO to prepare and
submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in
compliance with the above quoted rules.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
a) The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check in the amount of
P2,698,572.06 with complete supporting documents for the fund
transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund from General Fund to
Special Trust Fund, and
b) The Municipal Account to record the transfer of unexpended
LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the
appropriate accounting entries under General and Trust Fund.
i. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of
Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds
(Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash in
Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-
01-02-010).
ii. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank
– Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-
010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-
020).

We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat and
other concerned division officials to furnish the copies of the contracts
and purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after its
perfection together with all its necessary supporting documents
pursuant COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of
the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects
and activities undertaken during the year. We further recommend
that for the CY 2019, the GAD Plan and Budget should be prepared
and submitted immediately to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management ensure that the Accountant
prepare and submit to the Auditor monthly the BRS together with the
original copies of the debit/credit memo pursuant to COA Circular
No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for timely verification of the cash in
bank account.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a
General/Subsidiary Ledger for the account Cash in Bank per fund
and record on it all the transactions affecting the said account for
proper monitoring of the availability of cash at a given time.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer
to keep and maintain a separate Cashbook or Check Disbursement
Record or its equivalent solely for all transactions involving Trust
Fund pursuant to the provisions of the Cash Examination Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Report of
Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) on a monthly basis in
its prescribed form.

We further recommended that Management require the inventory of
the accountable forms for official receipts, prepare its RAAF and
submit it immediately to the Auditor..

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer
to strictly comply with the provisions of the law in the preparation
and submission of Reports of Collections and Deposits and its
supporting documents like the Official Receipts and Validated Deposit
Slips on a monthly basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of
Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together
with all the supporting liquidating instruments every 5th day of
the ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the
NGAS for LGUs.
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately review and
evaluate the submitted RODs and its attachments and then
prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers for submission to the
Auditor.
c) Finally, due to the unreasonable delay, require the immediate
submission of all the reports to the Office of the Auditor and the
prompt submission thereafter.

We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies
provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing
immediate compliance with the audit suspensions and disallowances
pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated
September 15, 2009.
